key_departures.impact: Starting center for two seasons retired in February. Left a void at C with no players under contract at the position. Chargers were sacked a career-high 54 times in 2025 — O-line stability is critical.

Released March 4 saving ~$9.7M. Sole season in LA plagued by injuries and inconsistent play. Was the starting RG but never recaptured his Eagles form. Departure compounds interior OL need alongside Bozeman retirement.

2022 first-round pick signed 3yr/$49.5M ($32.4M gtd) with Cleveland. Played best football in 2025 — was the lone reliable OL during injury-riddled season. Departure means both starting guard spots are now vacant alongside the center spot.

Signed 4yr/$100M ($68M gtd) with Washington. After trade from Baltimore mid-2025, registered 7.5 sacks and a 17.3% pressure rate in 12 games with LAC — career-best form. Had 3 sacks in playoff game vs. NE. Chargers chose not to match the market. Major loss for the pass rush.
Released March 4 saving $4M. His role diminished throughout 2025 as Oronde Gadsden II emerged. Departure left Gadsden as the only TE on roster heading into FA.
Breakout 2025 season (1 INT, 7 PD). Had been a 1-year prove-it deal player who outperformed expectations. Status as of March 10 is unsigned. If he departs, creates a CB depth need.
Veteran leader and physical presence. Missed time in each of the past two seasons. Departure is a depth loss but does not meaningfully escalate a need given his age and role.
Missed most of 2025 with an Achilles injury. Was a depth/backup piece only. Omarion Hampton and Kimani Vidal (tendered) form the core of the backfield. Departure is inconsequential.

UFA as of March 11. Perception is he wants to return to LA. Status remains unsigned as of March 10. If he departs, creates modest WR depth need behind Ladd McConkey, but team is not reliant on Allen as a true WR1.

signings.analysis: Signed January 26 — well before FA opened. Three-year extension (up to $37.5M with incentives, $20M guaranteed) locks up the Chargers' best interior defender. Tart was a run-stuffing force with 12 run stops (7th in NFL) in 2025, played all 17 games. $10M AAV is fair mid-market value for a starting NT. Keeps IDL stable alongside young Jamaree Caldwell. Need fully addressed — IDL drops from concern to non-need.

One-year, $18M fully guaranteed deal — same structure as 2025. Mack is a future Hall of Famer who remains productive (5.5 sacks in 12 games in 2025) and an elite run defender. However, the 1-year structure is a bridge, not a long-term solution. At 35, he is not the future of the position. With Oweh departing to Washington on a 4yr/$100M deal, EDGE remains a critical need for 2027+. Tuli Tuipulotu (13.5 sacks, final year of deal) plus Mack gives them a pass rush for 2026, but the position is not resolved long-term. Need partially addressed for 2026 only.

Signed March 6, before free agency opened. Three-year, $30M deal fills the center vacancy created by Bradley Bozeman's retirement. Biadasz (2022 Pro Bowler) graded 15th in pass block win rate vs. Bozeman's 30th — a clear upgrade. However, both guard spots remain empty after Becton's release and Johnson's departure to Cleveland. IOL need is partially addressed at C but critical at G(x2). Still a high-priority draft/FA position.

Signed March 9 (legal tampering). Three-year, $24.3M ($17M guaranteed) — makes him the highest-paid blocking TE in the NFL. Kolar pairs with pass-catching Oronde Gadsden II to give the Chargers a two-TE system. He had 10 catches/142 yds/2 TDs in 2025 but is primarily a blocker — important for Mike McDaniel's run-heavy scheme and Omarion Hampton. GM Hortiz drafted Kolar in Baltimore; strong familiarity. TE need is substantially reduced but not fully gone — Gadsden is unproven as WR1-level pass catcher, and receiving TE depth remains thin.

Signed March 8. Two-year deal up to $7.5M. Fullback reuniting with OC Mike McDaniel after 4 seasons together in Miami. Fullback is a unique role — not a traditional RB need. Ingold is primarily a lead blocker and receiving option as a FB. This signing is scheme-driven for McDaniel's offense; it does not meaningfully affect the RB depth chart. Low positional need impact but high schematic importance.

Signed March 9 (legal tampering). Two-year, $13M ($9M gtd) deal. Former Patriots 2022 first-round pick reunites with OC Mike McDaniel; started all 14 games at RG for Miami in 2025. PFF grade was sub-60.0 in both pass and run blocking — below average. At $6.5M AAV, this is a depth-tier deal by market standards. Likely penciled in as starting RG but this is not a long-term starter solution. With Zion Johnson gone to Cleveland, the Chargers still desperately need a starting LG. IOL remains a critical need (1 of 2 guard spots filled marginally; the other still open).

Re-signed March 9 on a 1-year, $4.5M deal. Versatile swing lineman (can play G or T). Provides OL depth insurance behind Slater/Alt at tackle and could compete for a guard role. However, at 193 offensive snaps in 2025 and a mixed track record, this is a depth move, not a starter solution. Does not address the LG vacancy in any meaningful way.

Exclusive rights free agent tendered March 6 at league minimum. Vidal had a career year in 2025 with 643 rushing yards and 3 TDs. Pairs with Omarion Hampton in backfield. This was a formality — team controls his rights. Backfield depth is solid. No meaningful need impact.
Exclusive rights free agent extended March 6. RB3/practice squad level depth. No meaningful need impact.

recommended_need_adjustments.reasoning: Despite signing Biadasz (C, 3yr/$30M) and Strange (G, 2yr/$13M), IOL remains a tier-1 critical need. The center spot is adequately filled. But both guard spots were vacated — Becton was released, Johnson signed with Cleveland (3yr/$49.5M). Strange at RG is a depth-tier signing by market value (sub-60 PFF grades) and not a true solution. LG remains completely unaddressed. With Justin Herbert sacked 54 times in 2025, IOL is still the team's biggest structural priority for the draft. Penning (1yr/$4.5M) adds depth only. Team needs at least one starting-caliber guard — likely a high draft pick.

Khalil Mack re-signed (1yr/$18M fully guaranteed) provides continuity, and Tuli Tuipulotu (13.5 sacks, entering final year of deal) anchors the group. However, Odafe Oweh's departure to Washington (4yr/$100M) removes the team's best young pass rusher. Mack is 35 on a 1-year bridge deal — not the future. EDGE need does not drop; the 2026 season is covered but the position remains a legitimate draft target for long-term depth/succession planning. Tuipulotu extension looming adds urgency — if he walks after 2026, this becomes tier 1.

Kolar signing (3yr/$24.3M, $17M gtd) substantially reduces TE need. He pairs with Gadsden II (breakout pass-catcher) to give LAC a functional two-TE unit. McDaniel's offense uses a run-game-focused TE well, and Kolar's blocking is among the best in the league. Need drops to tier 3 — they could still add depth/development TE in mid-rounds but no longer need a starter-level TE addition.

Tart's extension (3yr/$30M, $20M gtd) secures the best interior defender on a team-friendly deal. He is the Chargers' run-stuffing anchor. Jamaree Caldwell (3rd-round 2025 pick) provides developmental depth. IDL drops to a luxury pick at best — only depth competition needed.

Benjamin St-Juste (breakout 2025 season) remains unsigned as of March 10. If he re-signs, CB stays at tier 3 or lower. If he walks, CB escalates to tier 2. Derion Kendrick and other depth remain, but the absence of a confirmed CB2 starter keeps this need alive. Monitor St-Juste negotiations closely — his fate determines whether CB moves up in the draft.

Ladd McConkey is the clear WR1. Keenan Allen (34) is unsigned but expected to return on a low-cost deal. WR depth is adequate for 2026 but lacks a true WR2 behind McConkey at the starter level. Not a top-tier draft need — more of a value addition in rounds 3-5 if the right prospect is available.
See above. Net: Mack retained but aging and 1-year only; Oweh lost to Washington. No change in tier for now, but this is a prime candidate to escalate post-draft if Tuipulotu extension stalls.

key_additions.headline: Run-stuffing DT anchor locked up early — keeps LAC's elite interior D intact heading into 2026
Future Hall of Famer returns for his 5th Chargers season; 1-year bridge while Tuipulotu extension looms
Pro Bowl center fills Bozeman's vacancy; graded 15th in pass-block win rate — a genuine upgrade at the position
NFL's highest-paid blocking TE pairs with Oronde Gadsden II to reboot LAC's tight end room under McDaniel
McDaniel system reunion; penciled in at RG but sub-starter quality — guard need persists at LG
Fullback reunites with OC Mike McDaniel; critical scheme piece for Herbert's new run-heavy West Coast offense
Versatile swing lineman retained as OL insurance; competes for guard starts with both spots vacant

key_losses.headline: Breakout pass rusher (7.5 sacks in 12 games, 3 sacks in playoff game) lost to massive Washington deal — biggest departure of the offseason
2022 first-round pick departs; his exit leaves the LG spot completely bare alongside pre-existing RG vacancy
Starting C retired in February, triggering the interior OL overhaul that defines this entire offseason for LAC
Cut after injury-plagued, inconsistent lone season in LA; scheme change under McDaniel made him a poor fit
Veteran TE released as LAC remakes the position room under new OC Mike McDaniel; Gadsden II is the new anchor
Breakout 2025 CB who outplayed his contract is testing the market; LAC secondary depth hinges on his status

notes: Keenan Allen (WR, age 34) and Benjamin St-Juste (CB) remain unsigned UFAs as of March 10, 2026. Both are expected to have decisions soon. Tart extension contract base is $30M (confirmed by OTC/Spotrac); $37.5M figure includes incentives. Biadasz guaranteed money (est. ~$20.7M) based on prior Commanders deal structure; official LAC guarantee not yet disclosed — flagged as estimate. Strange $9M guaranteed per Yahoo Sports/ESPN reporting.
